_comment: Per-chart narratives. CI (build_prompt.py -> GitHub Models -> write_narrative.py) overwrites these from live numbers; this committed copy is the fallback so the page always reads well. source = 'fallback' | 'github-models'.

notes.basket: The breakfast basket, an aggregate price index of key commodities including coffee, sugar, wheat, and orange juice indexed to 2015 = 100, currently stands at 132. This reflects an approximate 32% increase in prices since 2015. The recent climb in this index has been uneven across components and time, influenced by specific market factors rather than broad economic trends. This basket serves as a snapshot of global soft commodity prices relevant to a common consumer breakfast.
notes.movers: Among the items, coffee has seen the most pronounced increase at 93% since 2015, followed by orange juice at 22%, while wheat and sugar have increased modestly by 7% and 5% respectively. These movements are primarily driven by small, specialized markets sensitive to weather events and crop diseases, such as frosts and droughts affecting arabica coffee, and citrus greening plus hurricanes impacting orange juice. Such soft commodities trade in thin markets where supply shocks cause marked price volatility rather than reflecting general inflationary pressures.
notes.why: The headline rise in the basket is largely due to significant disruptions in weather-exposed crops like coffee and orange juice. Given the nature of these commodity markets, input prices can remain elevated over extended periods as weather patterns, disease, and growing conditions continue to exert influence on supply and price dynamics.
